chapter_title.en: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes

text: 5342 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلْجُسُورِ أَ يَضْمَنُ أَهْلُهَا شَيْئاً قَالَ "لاَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.5342 - In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman from one of our companions, from Abu Abdullah (as), he was asked about bridges and whether their owners are liable for anything. Imam (as) replied: "No."
